overview.description: AI-powered engineering performance measurement platform that measures coding output, quality, velocity, alignment and AI impact
overview.description_long: Navigara is the performance layer for modern engineering teams. It uses dedicated AI agents to analyze real code and tooling data — measuring velocity, quality, and alignment against strategic business goals. Founded by an Ex-CTO and Ex-Director of Engineering, Navigara helps engineering leaders prove AI ROI, compare internal teams and external vendors on objective metrics, and build performance baselines from Git history. Integrates natively with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps, and Jira.

history.origin_story: Founded by Jirka Bachel (Ex-CTO with 18+ years of technical leadership across Silicon Valley and Prague) and Peter Malina (Ex-Director of Engineering with 15+ years of experience). Started as an AI evaluation tool and evolved into a full engineering performance platform when Peter joined as Co-Founder.

key_milestones.event: Navigara founded as an AI evaluation tool by Jirka Bachel
Peter Malina joined as Co-Founder & CTPO; product evolved into full engineering analytics platform
Serving customers including Kiwi.com, GTO Wizard, FTMO, and multiple Czech tech companies

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Navigara's core value proposition — AI-generated insights from git and Jira data — is directly replicable by foundation models. Competitors could offer similar engineering analytics dashboards using GPT-4 or Claude with relatively thin integrations; no proprietary model or unique dataset moat is evident.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Engineering analytics is a real and growing market (LinearB, Jellyfish, Swarmia, Pluralsight Flow are established players), but also crowded. Navigara's focus on AI ROI measurement is timely given AI coding tools proliferation, which provides differentiation.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: No known external funding found; appears bootstrapped with a small team (1-10 employees). With a $1,500/month standard plan and free tier, revenue may still be limited at this early stage.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Early-stage bootstrapped startup with no known funding, operating in a competitive space where the core AI analytics capability can be commoditized by foundation models. Customer traction (Kiwi.com, FTMO) is a positive signal but runway and defensibility remain concerns.

metadata.notes: No CNAIP URL available. LinkedIn page for 'navigara' resolves to a different Danish company (navigara.dk). The navigara.com company is Prague-based per founder bio ('Silicon Valley and Prague') and Czech customer base. Company is fully remote with Prague HQ inferred. Founded year unknown — website copyright shows 2026 but this appears to be a recently launched product. No Glassdoor or media coverage found.
